title: Rightmove Sued for £1.5 Billion by UK Estate Agent Coalition
body: A coalition of UK estate agencies has filed a £1.5 billion class-action lawsuit against Rightmove, alleging the portal abused its market-dominant position to impose excessive subscription fees on the country's 20,000 estate agencies. The claimants, represented by competition law specialist Hausfeld, number approximately 4,000 agencies and argue that Rightmove's pricing has increased by 50% over five years with no commensurate improvement in lead quality or volume. The lawsuit follows a period of growing agent frustration with Rightmove's pricing strategy, which has continued despite the emergence of rival portals OnTheMarket and Zoopla. Rightmove currently commands over 80% market share of UK property searches. Legal observers say the case could take 3-5 years to resolve and, if successful, could fundamentally alter how dominant property portals price their subscription products across Europe.

title: Home search is going AI-first with Realtor.com as attention gets harder to earn
body: Realtor.com has completely overhauled its core search experience, removing the traditional filter panel as the primary entry point and replacing it with an AI conversation interface that interprets buyer intent in natural language. The platform asks buyers to describe what they are looking for in their own words and surfaces ranked listings based on semantic understanding of their preferences. Alongside the web redesign, Realtor.com has launched a native ChatGPT plugin that enables end-to-end property search through dialogue, without opening the Realtor.com website. The company's product team described the change as 'the biggest product bet in our 25-year history'. Early AB test data shows a 23% increase in qualified lead submissions compared to the filter-based experience. Realtor.com is the second-largest US real estate portal by traffic with approximately 68 million monthly visitors.

title: CoStar Group Launches Transformative AI Experience on Homes.com
body: CoStar Group unveiled a comprehensive redesign of Homes.com built around generative AI as the primary search and discovery mechanism. The new experience allows buyers to describe a property using natural language — including lifestyle preferences, neighbourhood characteristics, and future life plans — and receive curated matches drawn from CoStar's proprietary property data spanning 140 million US residential and commercial properties. The system goes beyond standard filter translation: it can answer follow-up questions, explain why a listing was included, and compare properties along dimensions the buyer has not explicitly mentioned. CoStar CEO Andy Florance said the launch represents 'the end of filter-based search as the primary property discovery mechanism'. The company reported that Homes.com reached 30 million monthly unique visitors in March 2026, up from 8 million a year ago.

title: Opendoor acquires Doma's closing, escrow and title technology stack
body: Opendoor has completed the acquisition of the technology assets of Doma Holdings, a publicly traded proptech company, including Doma's AI-powered title insurance underwriting platform, escrow automation suite, and closing workflow software. The acquisition is valued at approximately $150 million and was funded through cash on hand. Opendoor says it will integrate the Doma technology stack into its Sell+Close product, which it plans to make available to third-party real estate agents within 12 months, enabling any agent to offer clients a faster, lower-cost closing process. The transaction marks a strategic pivot for Opendoor from direct homebuying operations (iBuying) toward transaction infrastructure services. CEO Carrie Wheeler described the move as 'owning the closing stack to earn on every transaction, not just our own'.

title: Czech Regulator Forces Sreality Subscription Price Cut After Abuse of Dominance Ruling
body: The Czech Office for the Protection of Competition issued a ruling that Sreality, the dominant Czech real estate portal owned by Seznam, must reduce real estate agency subscription fees by 35% within 90 days. The regulator found that Sreality's market position — used by 95% of Czech real estate agents — enabled it to impose pricing that could not be sustained in a competitive market. Agency fees had risen 80% since 2018. The ruling does not impose a financial penalty because Seznam agreed to the pricing reset voluntarily, but the regulator warned it would impose fines of up to 10% of revenue for non-compliance. The Czech case is the first instance in the EU of a property portal being compelled to reduce subscription pricing through competition law, and legal observers say it may embolden regulators in other markets, particularly the UK where Rightmove faces a private lawsuit over similar pricing concerns.

title: Zillow launches Zillow Preview — pre-market listings visible to buyers before MLS
body: Zillow today introduced Zillow Preview, a new listing type that allows sellers and their agents to display a property to Zillow's 220 million monthly visitors before the home is officially listed on the MLS. Properties in Preview status are visible on Zillow's search results with a 'Coming Soon' badge but are excluded from third-party syndication sites such as Realtor.com and Homes.com. Buyers can save Preview listings and be notified when they go live. Sellers benefit from market interest data before committing to a list price. For brokerages, Preview keeps qualified buyers on the Zillow platform during the pre-list period. As of April 1, more than 60 brokerage firms covering 18 states have opted into the program. Zillow said Preview addresses a 'growing seller request' to 'test the market before going all-in'.

title: Lofty AI launches end-to-end real estate CRM with full AI pipeline automation
body: Lofty (formerly Chime Technologies) has released a completely rebuilt CRM platform that uses AI agents to automate the full sales workflow for real estate agents. Key capabilities include: autonomous lead follow-up via email and SMS based on engagement signals, AI-drafted offer letters generated from listing data and client preference history, automated viewing schedule coordination with calendar integration, and predictive pipeline scoring that identifies which leads are most likely to transact in the next 30 days. The system integrates with all major MLS data feeds and can manage up to 200 simultaneous leads without manual intervention. Pricing is $499 per agent per month, with enterprise plans for teams. Lofty says early users report a 40% reduction in administrative time. The launch comes as AI-native CRM competitors including Raya and Structurely are gaining traction with similar automation promises.

title: French Portal Rivalry Intensifies as Bien'ici, SeLoger, and Leboncoin Dispute Market Leadership
body: France's three major residential real estate portals are engaged in an escalating public dispute over traffic rankings, lead quality metrics, and advertising claims. Aviv Group's SeLoger published a white paper claiming its leads convert to successful viewings 15% more often than those from Bien'ici, prompting Bien'ici (part of Figaro Classifieds) to respond with counter-data accusing SeLoger of cherry-picking time periods. Leboncoin (part of Adevinta) separately issued a statement claiming that both competitors inflate their 'unique visitor' figures by counting app and web sessions as separate users. The dispute has spilled into advertising, with all three portals running campaigns directly comparing their products to competitors' in major French cities. Industry analysts say the rivalry is intensifying as the French market consolidates around these three platforms following the exit of Logic-Immo. Rightmove-backed OnTheMarket has indicated interest in a French market entry, which may further complicate the competitive picture.

title: New survey data shows AI search adoption by US homebuyers climbing fast
body: A nationally representative survey of 2,400 recent US homebuyers conducted by Bright MLS found that 38% used an AI-assisted search tool at some point during their most recent home purchase, up from 11% in the same period in 2024. Among buyers aged 18-34, adoption reached 61%. The survey found that 72% of AI search users said the technology 'better captured what I was looking for' compared to filter-based alternatives, and 58% said they found homes they would not have discovered using standard search. The data also showed that AI search users submitted offers 6 days faster on average than non-AI users. Zillow, Realtor.com, and Homes.com all appear in the survey as platforms where AI search was used. The survey was conducted in February 2026 with a margin of error of ±2.1%.

title: Dubizzle Group launches end-to-end property transaction platform in Dubai
body: Dubizzle Group, the operator of UAE real estate portal bayut.com and classifieds platform dubizzle.com, has launched a fully integrated property transaction product in Dubai. The platform enables buyers and sellers to handle the complete transaction within the Dubizzle ecosystem: property search, offer and counteroffer, title deed transfer application through the Dubai Land Department API, mortgage pre-approval with three partner banks, and digital document signing. Dubizzle charges a flat AED 15,000 (~$4,100) transaction fee on completion regardless of property value, replacing the traditional agency commission model. The company processed over 200 end-to-end transactions during an eight-month pilot phase, reporting a net promoter score of 68. CEO Haider Khan said the model is designed to capture value from the transaction rather than the listing.

title: Apartments.com Releases Multifamily Rent Growth Report for March 2026
body: CoStar Group's Apartments.com division released its monthly Multifamily Rent Growth Report for March 2026, showing that US rental price growth has decelerated to 1.2% year-over-year nationally, down from 3.8% in March 2025. The Sun Belt markets — including Austin, Phoenix, and Charlotte — showed the sharpest declines, with Austin recording a 0.8% year-over-year decrease. Major gateway cities including New York and Boston held at 2.5-3.0% growth. The report attributes the deceleration to a 'supply surge' of new apartment completions in 2024-2025, with CoStar estimating 650,000 new units delivered nationally over the 18-month period. The report expects rent growth to stabilise at 1.5-2.5% nationally by Q3 2026 as supply absorption completes.

title: Scout24 agrees to acquire Fotocasa and Habitaclia from Adevinta for €620 million
body: Scout24, the German operator of ImmoScout24 and AutoScout24, has announced a binding agreement to acquire Fotocasa and Habitaclia from Norwegian classifieds group Adevinta for €620 million in cash. Fotocasa is Spain's second-largest real estate portal with approximately 7 million monthly visitors; Habitaclia is number three with 5 million. Together they give Scout24 a leading position in the Spanish market and extend its European geographic footprint beyond Germany, Austria, and Switzerland. Scout24 CEO Tobias Hartmann said the acquisition fits the company's strategy of 'building the leading digital real estate ecosystem across key European markets'. The acquisition is subject to EU Directorate-General for Competition approval and is expected to close Q3 2026. Scout24 is financing the transaction through a combination of existing credit facilities and new debt.

title: Avito Jobs launches branded vacancy format — direct parallel for Real Estate vertical
body: Avito has introduced a premium branded vacancy format in its Jobs vertical that allows employers to display job listings with corporate branding elements including logo, colour scheme, header imagery, and an expanded company description section. The format is priced at a 3x premium over standard listings and has been adopted by 240 employers in the first 30 days of availability. Early data shows branded listings generate 2.1x more applications per view than standard listings. Avito's commercial team described the product as part of a broader push toward 'media-grade brand presence' within classifieds environments. The format is directly analogous to developer and agency branded pages in real estate verticals and the data provides internal benchmarks for pricing and performance expectations for a Real Estate equivalent.

title: Property Finder closes $400M growth equity round but bets on depth over expansion
body: Property Finder, the leading real estate portal in the UAE with 4.5 million monthly active users, announced the close of a $400 million growth equity financing round led by KKR, with participation from existing investors including General Atlantic. Unlike recent property technology fundraises, the company explicitly stated it will not use the capital to expand geographically into new markets. Instead, CEO Michael Lahyani said Property Finder will invest in three areas: building a verified listings infrastructure that confirms property status, ownership, and condition before publishing; developing an AI-powered property valuation product calibrated to the MENA market; and integrating mortgage pre-approval from UAE and Saudi banks directly into the listing flow. The company said it intends to reach profitability in 2026 without further capital raises.

title: Avito Adtech launches self-serve analytics dashboard with impression-level data for ad clients
body: Avito has launched a redesigned analytics product for clients of its advertising platform, Avito Adtech. The update provides advertising clients — primarily large retailers, automotive dealerships, and real estate developers — with real-time data on ad impressions, audience demographics, engagement rates, and conversion events at the individual campaign level. For the first time, clients can make bid adjustments and targeting changes directly from the dashboard without involving an account manager. Avito said the change is part of a multi-year platform evolution toward performance marketing transparency. The company noted that similar self-serve capabilities in comparable platforms (e.g., Yandex.Direct) have been shown to increase advertiser retention by 15-20% and average spend per client by 12%. The Real Estate vertical is identified as the highest-value vertical for Adtech and the primary intended beneficiary of the product.

title: Yandex Q4 Revenue Up 28% as Real Estate Sits Within AI Engine
body: Yandex reported Q4 2025 revenue growth of 28% year-over-year, with its real estate vertical embedded natively inside its YandexGPT AI search engine rather than operating as a separate property portal. The company said property listings are now surfaced through conversational AI responses to queries such as 'find me a two-bedroom apartment in Butovo under 12 million roubles' — generating a 31% increase in real estate session depth compared to filter-based search on its legacy vertical. Yandex's Head of Real Estate said the model is designed to eliminate the 'portal step' in the property search journey: users discover listings inside the AI answer without navigating to a dedicated site. The integrated AI-search-to-listing model is showing strong monetisation, with real estate CPCs up 19% quarter-over-quarter as advertisers bid for placement in AI-surfaced answers. Yandex described the architecture — real estate as a native vertical inside a generalist AI engine — as its primary competitive moat against specialist property portals like CIAN and Avito Real Estate.
